title: SPARTA Crypto-D1 Local Data Acquisition Authorization / Freeze Plan v1
research_objective: Define EXACTLY what the operator must MANUALLY provide later, WHERE it should go, and WHICH checks must pass before the Crypto-D1 Backtest Runner v1 is allowed to use it. This bundle is an authorization / freeze PLAN only -- it does not fetch data, does not connect to any exchange, does not produce any real dataset file, does not authorize trading or backtest execution.

target_assets.label: Solana
target_assets.rationale: Different vol / drawdown profile; prevents BTC-only overfit.

timeframe.bar_close_convention: UTC 00:00:00 close; left-closed / right-open documented per dataset manifest.

approved_data_scope.quote_currency_consistency_required: Within a single dataset_id, every row for the same symbol MUST share the same quote_currency. Cross-stablecoin conversions require an explicit FX series.
approved_data_scope.source_type: operator-approved historical OHLCV CSV provider (offline export only)
approved_data_scope.time_range_policy: Operator-chosen; must be RECORDED EXACTLY in the per-dataset manifest's time_start / time_end fields.

operator_manual_steps: Step 1: Operator MANUALLY selects an approved historical OHLCV CSV source whose terms of service explicitly permit research use. NO automated discovery.

Step 2: Operator MANUALLY exports / downloads daily OHLCV CSV files for BTC, ETH, SOL (whichever subset is in scope) under the operator's own credentials; SPARTA's runtime never sees those credentials.
Step 3: Operator MANUALLY normalizes the CSV(s) so that the file header matches the Bundle 12 required_csv_schema EXACTLY (column names, casing, UTF-8 encoding).
Step 4: Operator MANUALLY mints a dataset_id matching the pattern 'CRYPTO_D1_SPOT_<SYMBOLS_TAG>_V<NNN>' and a dataset_version (V001, V002, ...).
Step 5: Operator MANUALLY places the CSV file(s) at the declared storage_path_plan ('data/crypto_d1_research/<dataset_id>/<dataset_version>/'). No network call is made by SPARTA.

Step 6: Operator MANUALLY authors a per-dataset manifest.json that satisfies tools/crypto_d1_dataset_manifest_check.py (extended for per-dataset use) and pins protocol_version, data_contract_version, manifest_version.
Step 7: Operator MANUALLY computes sha256 of every file in the dataset folder and writes them to CHECKSUMS.txt.
Step 8: Operator MANUALLY writes FREEZE_RECORD.txt recording freeze timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC), operator name + tool label, and the pinned protocol+data_contract+manifest+qa-spec versions.
Step 9: Operator MANUALLY runs the future QA harness (Bundle 14 spec) once it is implemented, and reviews qa_report.json.
Step 10: Operator MANUALLY accepts QA_PASS, or attaches an explicit operator-acceptance note for QA_WARN, before authorizing the runner.
Step 11: Operator MANUALLY runs the backtest runner against the FROZEN, QA-approved dataset: `python tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py run --data-dir <PATH> --out-dir <PATH>`.

Step 12: Operator MANUALLY reviews the runner's emitted crypto_d1_backtest_report.json + .md (research-only); the runner NEVER emits PASS in v1, so any acted-upon decision is the operator's explicit judgment AFTER review.

required_csv_schema.header_must_match_exactly: Column names, casing, and order MUST match the Bundle 12 data contract; the backtest runner rejects files whose header is missing any required column.

expected_file_layout.purpose: Documents the EXPECTED filenames a future operator-supplied dataset folder should contain. NO real files are created in this bundle.
expected_file_layout.checksums_txt: CHECKSUMS.txt -- one 'sha256  <filename>' line per dataset file.
expected_file_layout.freeze_record_txt: FREEZE_RECORD.txt -- freeze timestamp, operator + tool label, pinned versions.
expected_file_layout.qa_report_json: qa_report.json -- emitted by the future QA harness (Bundle 14 spec); MUST populate all 26 fields declared in qa_freeze_spec_v1.QA_report_schema.required_fields.

storage_path_plan.no_remote_paths: All paths are local repo-relative; no s3://, gs://, http(s)://, ftp://, file://remote-host, or other remote scheme is permitted in any future per-dataset manifest.
storage_path_plan.storage_root_created_only_when_operator_: Even the parent directory 'data/crypto_d1_research/' is NOT created by this authorization plan; it appears only when the operator manually places a real dataset.
storage_path_plan.gitignore_recommendation_for_future_oper: If real OHLCV data is later acquired, add 'data/crypto_d1_research/' to .gitignore unless the operator explicitly wants to track frozen datasets in git (typically NOT recommended for size + provenance reasons).

QA_freeze_requirements.qa_status_must_be: QA_PASS or QA_WARN with explicit operator-acceptance note
checks_required_in_qa_report: B_timestamp blocking checks (UTC, no weekday-only, no duplicate, no silent forward-fill)
C_OHLCV blocking checks (positive OHLC; high>=max(o,c,l); low<=min(o,c,h); close not missing)
D_volume blocking checks (non-negative; zero-volume policy applied)
E_symbol_source blocking checks (BTC/ETH/SOL canonical; quote_currency consistency; row-level source_id)
F_fee_slippage blocking checks (fee + slippage assumptions present and sourced)
G_freeze blocking checks (FROZEN; CHECKSUMS.txt sha256-verified; FREEZE_RECORD.txt present)

checksum_requirements.checksums_must_match_at_runner_startup: The backtest runner (or its future per-dataset validator) MUST verify that each file's current sha256 equals the recorded CHECKSUMS.txt entry before any backtest result is produced.
checksum_requirements.freeze_record_txt_format: Plain-text key:value lines including: freeze_timestamp_utc, operator, protocol_version, data_contract_version, manifest_version, qa_freeze_spec_version, backtest_plan_version, runner_version.

forbidden_actions: Fetch data from any live exchange API.
Connect SPARTA's runtime to any exchange or vendor over the network.
Use an embedded API key, OAuth token, or .env credential.
Use any private key or exchange account credential.
Scrape any venue HTML / WebSocket / undocumented endpoint.
Use any cloud fetcher (s3://, gs://, http(s)://, ftp://, file://remote-host) as a source.
Install or schedule any daemon / cron / background job to perform acquisition.
Run the backtest runner from this bundle.
Promote crypto_d1_protocol to ACTIVE / STRONG by this bundle's existence alone.
Use any synthetic / mock-priced data as evidence.
Send a Telegram / email / Slack notification from this bundle's runtime.
approval_gates: Explicit written operator authorization BEFORE any data acquisition step is performed.
Exact source named (vendor / venue / archive name) in the manifest's source_name field.
Exact symbols named (subset of {BTC, ETH, SOL}) in the manifest's assets field.
Exact time window named (time_start, time_end -- both ISO-8601 UTC).
Exact storage path named (data/crypto_d1_research/<dataset_id>/<dataset_version>/).
Exact dataset_id named (CRYPTO_D1_SPOT_<SYMBOLS_TAG>_V<NNN>).
data_contract_version pinned (crypto_d1_data_contract_v1 or later).
dataset_manifest_spec_version pinned (crypto_d1_dataset_manifest_v1 or later).
qa_freeze_spec_version pinned (crypto_d1_qa_freeze_spec_v1 or later).
protocol_version pinned (crypto_d1_protocol_v1 or later).
CHECKSUMS.txt created with sha256 per file.
FREEZE_RECORD.txt created with freeze timestamp + operator + pinned versions.
manifest.json created and passes tools/crypto_d1_dataset_manifest_check.py.
QA / freeze qa_report.json created and passes the Bundle 14 spec.
QA_status is QA_PASS OR ('QA_WARN' with an explicit operator-acceptance note attached).
No credentials of any kind embedded in the dataset, manifest, or supporting files.
No trading permissions, exchange-account references, or order-routing identifiers anywhere in the dataset.
No live trading authorization.
No paper-order execution authorization.
No broker connection authorization.
allowed_next_steps: Operator manually executes the 12 operator_manual_steps above; no SPARTA-side automation is invoked.
Operator runs `python tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py validate-config` to confirm cost bounds.
Operator runs `python tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py show-plan` to review the pre-registered plan.
Operator runs `python tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py run --data-dir <PATH> --out-dir <PATH>` against the FROZEN, QA-approved dataset; emitted JSON+MD reports are research-only.

Author **Crypto-D1 Baseline Results Report v1** AFTER the operator has manually supplied data AND the runner has produced output (separately authorized; documents what the operator-reviewed run looks like).
Author **Crypto-D1 Data QA Runtime Tool v1** (if the operator decides the QA runner MUST exist before any further backtest; code bundle; recommend Codex).
forbidden_next_steps: Trade live or paper based on a future runner output.
Connect SPARTA's runtime to any exchange or vendor over the network.
Promote crypto_d1_protocol to ACTIVE / STRONG without a separate operator decision.
Schedule a daemon / cron / background process that touches the runner.
Install or read any API key, OAuth token, or .env credential.
Use any synthetic / mock-priced data as evidence.
Commit large frozen datasets to git without explicit operator instruction; the storage root is expected to be gitignored by default.
required_future_artifacts: Per-dataset manifest.json conforming to Bundle 13 schema.
Per-dataset CHECKSUMS.txt with sha256 per file.
Per-dataset FREEZE_RECORD.txt with freeze timestamp + operator + pinned versions.
Per-dataset qa_report.json conforming to Bundle 14 QA_report_schema (26 required fields).
Per-strategy backtest report.json + report.md emitted by tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py after the operator runs the runner against the FROZEN dataset.
Crypto-D1 Baseline Results Report v1 (separately authorized; only after operator has supplied data and the runner has produced output).
Optional: Crypto-D1 Data QA Runtime Tool v1 (code bundle; only if the operator decides the QA runner must exist as code before any further backtest; recommend Codex).
safety_boundaries: No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No paper-order execution. No order placement.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this plan's runtime.
No data fetch in this bundle. No backtest execution. No dataset processing. No real dataset files or data directory created in this bundle.
Do not modify paper / live execution files.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this authorization plan alone.

Data acquisition does not imply edge. Clean data does not imply profit. Running the backtest does not authorize trading. Backtest output will still require separate review. No paper/live trading is authorized. Crypto trend ideas are not profitable until tested with full costs AND forward-validated under a separately authorized future plan. A good historical chart does not imply future returns. 24/7 crypto session handling; weekday-only filters forbidden.
no_profit_claim_policy: Data acquisition does not imply edge.
Clean data does not imply profit.
Running the backtest does not authorize trading.
Backtest output will still require separate review.
No data fetch is authorized by this bundle.
Crypto trend ideas are not profitable until tested.
A good historical chart does not imply future returns.
